articles.title: For Australia, the end of the Iran war might just be the start

children.content: The federal government is turning its mind to what Australian support in the Middle East might look like after a ceasefire.

image.altText: Two men and a woman in suits stand at a podium speaking to journalists with the Australian flag in the background.

children.content: Anthony Albanese has announced new fuel security powers to get "fuel restocked" so Australia is "ready for what may come". Follow live.

items.title: 'They got it all wrong': The miscalculation the US and Israel made over Iran

children.content: Donald Trump is deploying thousands of marines and extra battleships to the Middle East, while also claiming to be close to a deal with Iran to end the war. So is he set to wind down his war with Iran, or is he thinking of escalating?

image.altText: US President Donald Trump delivers a speech on a stage in front of a microphone.

items.title: Trump under pressure to find an off-ramp amid oil and munitions crisis

children.content: While markets struggle to price a sudden collapse in oil supply military analysts are sounding the alarm on the rate countries are burning through weapons stockpiles.

image.altText: Donald Trump looking up in an inquisitive manner

items.title: US 'on verge' of ending Iran war as Rubio demands allies lead postwar coalition

children.content: Secretary of State Marco Rubio tells G7 leaders to ready a postwar coalition to secure the Strait of Hormuz, saying the US-Israeli war on Iran could conclude within weeks.

image.altText: Marco Rubio addresses reporters on the tarmac infront of a plane.

items.title: This man went viral after surviving Bondi. Then the internet took a dark turn

children.content: Arsen Ostrovsky was on the ground, bleeding, at the Bondi attack when he decided to take a selfie. Within 2 hours it was circulating on the internet. In less than a day, it had become fodder for a global conspiracy theory.

image.altText: A middle-aged man with a white bandage on his head. Telegram messages and images fade in behind him.

shredded gazebo with blown over fence

articles.title: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle causes outages at major WA gas facilities

children.content: Gas giants Woodside, Santos and Chevron confirm they have suffered production outages at its gas as a result of Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

image.altText: An aerial shot of the Gorgon gas project on the coast of Western Australia.

articles.title: Northern WA picks up the pieces in the wake of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle weakens as it travels south through WA's Wheatbelt, leaving a trail of destruction in its wake.

image.altText: A tree uprootes and concrete slab distrubed at a school.
Timelapse of Tropical Cyclone Narelle's path

children.content: The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting the WA coast.

items.title: Mapping Israel's destruction of Lebanese bridges

children.content: Israel's expanded invasion of southern Lebanon was at least 12 days in the making, with bridges targeted to effectively cut off the south from the country's centre.

items.title: Tiger Woods arrested following car crash near Florida home

children.content: Golf legend Tiger Woods is arrested in Florida after refusing to submit a urine sample following a car crash near his Florida home.

items.title: Do we actually have the workforce for the $4b NDIS alternative?

children.content: Months away from the start of a huge shift in disability support for children, the workforce expected to deliver it has concerns about its readiness.

items.title: Dementia is Australia's top killer, yet most still miss the early signs

children.content: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand resting on an elderly woman's hands.

items.title: Isolated school students make sacrifices amid fuel crisis

children.content: Ruben Driver has a round trip of 4,000 kilometres to reach his boarding school. The cost of fuel is placing his family under immense pressure to get him to class.

image.altText: A boy standing in front of a plane

items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

gallery.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.
A man with grey hair wearing a pink shirt leans on a pastry fridge behind the counter of a regional general store.

items.title: 'Built on deception': How one online travel site is fleecing customers

children.content: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

children.content: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

items.title: Ranked number one on the terrorism index, this nation struck back

children.content: As global attention remains fixed on the sprawling war in the Middle East, another conflict is edging towards a dangerous threshold — one between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Taliban-controlled Afghanistan.

image.altText: A Taliban soldier operates an anti-aircraft gun on a clear day.

items.title: EPA, developer defend records over contentious WA beach development

children.content: The City of Busselton says the watchdog has ignored its own rules after giving the Smiths Beach development the green light, but Fini Group says its work was "rigorous".

items.title: Foran named interim coach after Manly sack Seibold following 0-3 start

children.content: Club legend Kieran Foran is appointed Manly's interim coach, after the Sea Eagles sacked Anthony Seibold just three games into the 2026 NRL season.

image.altText: Sea Eagles assistant coach Kieran Foran instructing players on the field

items.title: 'It's about fairness': Australian Iranians hit back at 'racist' visa ban

children.content: Thousands of Iranian travellers have been barred from entering Australia under a government ban targeting people who might overstay their visas, prompting criticism by refugee advocates.

items.title: Iran's soccer team honours victims of deadly airstrike on primary school

children.content: Iran soccer players hold small backpacks to honour the victims of a deadly missile strike on a girls' primary school, before a friendly against Nigeria.

image.altText: Iran's players sing national anthem holding school bags

items.title: A century ago, a Liberal Party disintegrated, and there are lessons for its namesake

children.content: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

image.altText: A painting depicting a crowd in a street

items.title: 'Stay in your lane': Experts urge against following running influencer advice

children.content: Recently, online discussion has turned to what some perceive to be a spate of influencers getting injured.

items.title: Savannah Guthrie to return to Today show after mother's disappearance

children.content: Savannah Guthrie is scheduled to return to her job as a co-anchor of NBC's Today show on April 6, more than two months after her 84-year-old mother's unresolved abduction.

image.altText: Savannah Guthrie (left) with her mum (right).

items.title: Blueberry industry under scrutiny amid rapid growth

children.content: Australians are eating and growing more blueberries than ever before, but questions are being asked about how production of the fruit is being regulated.

image.altText: Blueberries on a bush in greenhouse.

items.title: WA minister wants reforms to local council laws

children.content: Local Government Minister Hannah Beazley says reforms are needed to prevent local councils from quitting just a few months into the job.

items.title: Signs point to measles cases spreading undetected in NSW

children.content: An outbreak of measles in south-east Asia has fuelled a surge in NSW cases — but the state's biggest cause for concern is five infections believed to have been spread undetected in the community.

image.altText: Gloved hands prepare a needle for vaccination.

items.title: Bush artist 'Pro' Hart remembered 20 years after death

children.content: On March 28, 2006, Broken Hill lost one of its most renowned residents in internationally acclaimed artist Kevin "Pro" Hart. He was, and still is, considered a widely popular artist, but his recognition was divisive to different audiences.

items.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects

children.content: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

items.title: The next step after treaty is here — and there's a lot at stake

children.content: Victoria's treaty elections are underway, with successful candidates taking on new responsibilities. But uncertainty remains, with the opposition sticking to its pledge to scrap the agreement if elected in November.
